name: Bow Tie Pasta with Blackened Scallops
recipeIngredient: Fresh basil, chopped, 2 tbsp - or dried basil, 1 tb
recipeInstructions:
Note: Seafood Stock can be made from fish-flavored bouillon cubes, available in most supermarkets.
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat.
2. Make the pepper sauce: Puree roasted peppers, shallots, garlic, and vinegar until smooth. Add seafood stock and basil, then blend. Transfer to a small saucepan, cover, and warm on the lowest setting.
3. For cilantro pesto: Process walnuts, garlic, and cilantro in a food processor. Add seafood stock through the feed tube, drizzle lemon juice, and form a paste. Whisk in more stock.
4. Cook pasta to desired tenderness (8-10 minutes), then drain.
5. Season scallops with blackening seasoning, paprika, and thyme. Heat a heavy frying pan over medium heat, oil it, and sear scallops 2 minutes per side. Reduce heat, squeeze lemon over scallops, cover, and cook 5 more minutes until firm.
6. Return pasta to pot, stir in cilantro pesto, divide among bowls, top with scallops, and pour pepper sauce over them.
